text.title: Seven Performance Domains (PMBoK 8) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: LLM output contains 'Integration Management' or 'Quality Management' as domain names.
antipattern.root-cause: These are PMBoK 6 knowledge area names; output is anchored to wrong edition.
antipattern.fix: Reject and regenerate with PMBoK 8 vocabulary pinned in system prompt.

antipattern.symptom: Agent output lists eight domains (adds Quality or Communications as standalone).
antipattern.fix: Validate output length == 7; reject and re-prompt with canonical seven.

antipattern.symptom: Domain status colours drift to 'green' over time without human sampling.
antipattern.root-cause: Agents self-validate by confirming artefact existence, not quality.
antipattern.fix: Calibrate by sampling 1 in 5 domains for human review each cycle.

antipattern.symptom: Agents underweight Procurement on vendor-heavy projects (demoted to appendix in PMBoK 8).
antipattern.root-cause: Vendor risk under-attended on contractor-heavy projects.
antipattern.fix: Force a vendor-risk question into the audit; treat Procurement signal as a Risk-domain input.
